title: coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia
intro: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia, commonly known as Japanese coriaria, is a plant species that belongs to the Coriariaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihistamine properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions, allergies, and wounds. The therapeutic actions of Japanese coriaria include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing through its bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The bioactive constituents of Japanese coriaria contribute to its therapeutic actions, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health and potential interactions with other medications.
benefits: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia has many benefits, such as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, thanks to its anxiolytic and antidepressant properties. This plant has also been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. Additionally, Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia has been found to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential natural remedy for infections and wounds. By harnessing the benefits of this plant, individuals can improve their overall well-being, increase their quality of life, and enhance their physical and mental health.
actions: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in conditions like arthritis, and improve overall well-being. Its antimicrobial and antifungal properties also aid in wound healing, preventing infections, and promoting a healthy immune system. Additionally, its antispasmodic and sedative effects can help manage stress and anxiety, promoting a restful sleep and improved mental clarity. By harnessing these therapeutic actions, individuals can enjoy improved physical and mental health, enhanced daily productivity, and a greater sense of overall well-being.
constituents: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents include coriarianine, coriariopicrin, and quercetin, among others, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The medicinal properties of Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia can improve people's daily lives by reducing inflammation and pain, protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage, and preventing the growth of microorganisms that can cause infections. For example, the flavonoid quercetin can help to alleviate symptoms of allergies and asthma, while the phenolic acid ferulic acid can help to protect against neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
parts: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, which contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant are also used medicinally and contain compounds like coriariin A, which has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity. The bark of Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments and contains constituents such as phenolic acids, which have been reported to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's berries are used in folk medicine to treat various conditions and contain anthocyanins, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that are used for medicinal purposes. The tea made from the plant is used to treat conditions like fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, while the tincture is used as a diuretic and to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. The infusion, on the other hand, is used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Additionally, Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia extracts are also used in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
side_effects: Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high tannin content. Excessive consumption may also cause kidney damage, as the plant's toxic compounds can strain the kidneys and lead to renal failure. In addition, improper use of Coriaria japonica subsp. intermedia can result in allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives, in some individuals. Furthermore, its toxic alkaloids can cause neurological symptoms, including headaches, dizziness, and even seizures, if ingested in large quantities.
